hero.description: Explore water access, income inequality, and development indicators across the continent — with interactive maps, 23-year trend data, and country-level analytics built for researchers and policymakers.

ldnPlatform.heading: Africa's Integrated Land Degradation Monitoring System
ldnPlatform.subtitle: LDN Platform Africa
ldnPlatform.description: Monitoring land degradation in Africa to support sustainable land management and Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN).
ldnPlatform.tagline: Access data. Analyse trends. Support decision-making.

overview.title: Overview of the Africa Land Degradation Monitoring System | LDN Platform Africa
overview.paragraph1: Land degradation is one of the most pressing environmental challenges in Africa, affecting agricultural productivity, water availability, biodiversity, and climate resilience. According to global assessments, Africa loses millions of hectares of productive land every year due to soil erosion, desertification, and unsustainable land use practices.
overview.paragraph2: This platform provides a centralized and integrated system for monitoring land degradation in Africa, enabling countries, institutions, and stakeholders to:
overview.item1: Track progress toward Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN).
overview.item2: Monitor SDG indicator 15.3.1 (proportion of degraded land).
overview.item3: Access harmonized geospatial datasets and indicators.
overview.closingParagraph: By combining Earth Observation-based data, national information, and analytical tools, the platform supports evidence-based decision-making at national and regional levels.

keyFacts.heading: Land Degradation in Africa: Key Facts
keyFacts.fact1: of Africa's productive land is degraded (UNCCD estimates).
keyFacts.fact2: Nearly 3 million hectares of forest are lost each year in Africa.
keyFacts.fact3: Over 40% of the population depends on degraded land for livelihoods.
keyFacts.fact4: Land degradation costs African economies billions of USD annually.
keyFacts.closingText: Monitoring and reversing these trends is essential to achieving sustainable development and climate resilience.

roleOfLDN.heading: The Role of the LDN Platform in Africa
roleOfLDN.description: The LDN Platform Africa serves as a centralized system for land degradation monitoring in Africa, integrating environmental, geospatial, and hydrological data into a single access point.
roleOfLDN.item1: Improving the accuracy of land degradation monitoring at national and continental levels.
roleOfLDN.item2: Supporting sustainable land-use planning and agricultural management.
roleOfLDN.item3: Facilitating reporting on Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN) and SDG 15.3.1.
roleOfLDN.item4: Strengthening evidence-based decision-making for environmental policies and restoration strategies.
roleOfLDN.closingParagraph: By transforming data into actionable insights, the platform enables stakeholders to better understand land degradation dynamics and take effective measures toward achieving Land Degradation Neutrality in Africa.

keyAssets.heading: Key assets of Africa's Integrated Land Degradation Monitoring System
keyAssets.description: This system bridges the gap between data and decision-making by providing:
keyAssets.item1: Reliable and harmonized datasets across Africa.
keyAssets.item2: Tools for monitoring land degradation trends over time.
keyAssets.item3: Support for national and international reporting frameworks.
keyAssets.item4: A shared space for knowledge, collaboration, and innovation.
keyAssets.closingText: Enabling stakeholders to move from data to action.

audience.heading: Who is this platform for?
audience.description: This platform is designed for a wide range of stakeholders involved in land management and environmental monitoring:

policymakers.label: Policymakers
policymakers.desc: tracking LDN targets and national reporting.

internationalOrgs.label: International Organizations
internationalOrgs.desc: supporting SDG monitoring and climate action.

continentalData.title: Continental-scale land degradation data
continentalData.desc: Derived from EO-derived and harmonized datasets across Africa.

geoportal.title: Interactive Geoportal for spatial analysis
geoportal.desc: Explore land cover, productivity, and soil indicators through dynamic maps.

dashboards.title: Dashboards for LDN and SDG 15.3.1 monitoring
dashboards.desc: Analyse trends, compare countries, and track progress over time.

resources.title: Resources and methodologies
resources.desc: Access guidelines, tools, and documentation for land degradation assessment.

successStories.title: Success stories and best practices
successStories.desc: Learn from real-world solutions implemented across African countries.

dataSources.text: Data sources include OSS, ESA, UNCCD, FAO, EO products, and national datasets.

held1.title: Second webinar dedicated to the 2026 Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN) reporting cycle for 22 French-speaking countries.

held2.title: First webinar dedicated to the 2026 Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN) reporting cycle for 22 English-speaking countries.

held3.title: Workshop – Launch of the Land Degradation and Sustainable Land Management Monitoring System in support of the Great Green Wall Initiative.

held4.title: Workshop to strengthen the reporting of Land Degradation Neutrality, on the sidelines of the Desertif'Actions 2026 International Summit.

upcoming1.title: Regional capacity-building workshop on reporting for Francophone African countries (Annex I Parties).

upcoming2.title: Regional capacity-building workshop on reporting for Anglophone African countries (Annex I Parties).

oss.heading: The Sahara and Sahel Observatory (OSS)
oss.subtitle: A UNCCD strategic partner and a regional hub for reporting on Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN)
oss.intro: The Sahara and Sahel Observatory (OSS) is an Africa-focused international organization supporting countries in addressing challenges related to land degradation, climate change, biodiversity, and water resource management.

designatedHub.text: In April 2026, OSS was designated as a Regional Support Hub to support African countries in reporting on:

technicalEngine.heading: A Technical Engine for Africa
technicalEngine.description: OSS is supporting evidence-based policymaking and contributing to accelerate progress toward Land Degradation Neutrality by leveraging:
technicalEngine.item3: Experiences from sustainable land restoration regional initiatives.

capacityBuilding.heading: A Lead of Capacity-Building and Technical Support
capacityBuilding.description: In partnership with organizations such as UNCCD, RCMRD, OSS supports:
capacityBuilding.item3: English and French-speaking countries' focal points and key experts.
